what_it_does: It is an interpretive ledger entry that documents and assesses the Build.jsx React canvas, summarizing its 4-stage brief-to-plan-to-parallel-execution-to-compose pipeline, its specialist sub-agents, refinement modes, conventions, concerns, themes, and embedding summaries.
observations: Identifies the source as project `claude-ds`, path `app/canvases/Build.jsx`, and model `opus`.
Provides a dense functional summary of the `Build` canvas as a 4-stage planner driven by `window.CV_AI.execute` and `CV_AI.complete`.
Lists seven seed brief templates: brochure, palette, icons+colors, voice, slide, widget, and wizard.
Describes a stage machine via `useState` stages 1-4 with busy flags for planning, generating, composing, and refining.
Documents `generatePlan()`, `runGeneration()`, `runSubtask` dispatch, and two refinement paths (`refinePart` and `refine`).
Notes external global dependencies such as `window.viDraftWidget`, `window.viDraftWizard`, `window.WS_viDraft`, `window.CV_TYPES_VI`, `window.WidgetRender`, and `window.CV_TYPES_PROMPT`.
Records brand constraints embedded in prompts, including 24x24 1.5px stroke icons and a warm ivory/ink/gold/bronze palette.
Includes an inventory of inline subcomponents and a `SUBTASK_META` tone/label mapping per subtask kind.
Notes defensive JSON parsing helpers `parseJson` and `parseJsonLoose` with regex fallbacks for fenced or prose-wrapped replies.
Flags a stray `}/>` after a self-closed `Output` element at line 618 as malformed JSX.
The `symbols` array is empty even though the entry describes many inline subcomponents.
standouts: A user-editable plan-review stage between brief and execution, where Vi proposes subtasks and the user edits or removes them before anything runs.
Multi-granularity refinement covering single item, whole section, and composition-only scopes, with the composer always rerun afterward.
Triage-driven refinement where a model decides which subtasks to re-run from the user's free-text change request.
conventions: Follows a generate -> propose/plan -> review -> adopt flow.
Parses model JSON defensively with raw JSON, curly-brace regex, and square-bracket regex fallbacks.
Routes model calls through named `CV_AI.execute` capabilities and `CV_AI.complete` rather than a raw model client.
Sources the brand voice from `window.CV_AI.get('voice.conceptv').text` instead of inlining it.
Aliases React hooks with a `_b` suffix for the shared bundle.
Restores the previous result and toasts on bad refinement responses instead of blanking a section.
concerns: The claim about a stray `}/>` at line 618 cannot be verified from this interpretive file alone because no source code is included.
Several runners depend on external globals whose contracts are not defined here, and they throw or fall back when absent.
Many catch blocks around composer reruns are empty, swallowing errors silently.
The icons, colors, copy, and composer prompts each embed the brand description as a string literal within the source file.
Open questions remain about what `build.plan`, `build.triage`, and other `CV_AI` capabilities are and how they differ from `CV_AI.complete`.
The empty `symbols` array underrepresents the many inline subcomponents described in the observations.
notes: An interpretive snapshot of the Build canvas that captures its planner pipeline, specialist runner taxonomy, refinement semantics, brand constraints, external dependencies, and open questions for downstream fusion.
questions: What capabilities are `build.plan`, `build.triage`, `build.icons`, `build.colors`, and `build.copy` in `CV_AI`, and how do they differ from `CV_AI.complete`?
Is the stray `}/>` at line 618 a typo that would break compilation, or is it stripped by a build step before loading?
Why is the `symbols` array empty when the entry describes many inline subcomponents?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the entry states it is a cross-canvas Vi planner and the summary describes a brief-to-plan-to-parallel-subtasks-to-compose pipeline with refinement and adoption.
apparent_themes: Orchestration of parallel specialist sub-agents from one brief
Brand-consistent generation through embedded voice, palette, and icon constraints
intention_signals: To turn Vi into an end-to-end maker: one natural-language brief becomes a reviewable, parallel, on-brand build that can be adopted, refined at multiple scopes, and saved as a reusable parameterized template.
novelty: A staged planner UI with an editable subtask tree, parallel specialist execution, and triage-based refinement that re-plans which subtasks to rerun from free text.
contribution: Contributes an interpretive record of the cross-canvas Build planner, preserving its 4-stage flow, specialist runner taxonomy, refinement semantics, brand constraints, external dependencies, and open questions for downstream fusion.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger entry documenting the Build.jsx React canvas as a 4-stage planner that turns a brief into reviewable parallel specialist subtasks, runs them, composes the result, and supports adoption, refinement, and template saving.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the design intent and structure of the Build canvas so downstream queries can understand its planner flow, specialist agents, and refinement model.
